posts.author: murali603
posts.text: FOCO stores would be 50% in FY27 and they are still not hedged 100%. I hope they will see some inventory losses in this quarter as well due to correction in prices and come me up with some cooked story about margins drop like they did in Q4. On the otherhand, demand seems to sluggish on the ground at store level.

posts.author: atishay_jain1
posts.text: That would be speculative. And promoter are not selling their stake, they will be diluting it within next 15 months via QIP to achieve 75% maximum promoter shareholding norm.

posts.author: new_investor
posts.text: Since the general consensus is that the management has not been very honest with the guidance and they want to rally up their stocks before the promoters sell their shares do you think it is a good bet to buy up the stock now - in hopes that we can exit in this same spike ?

posts.author: atishay_jain1
posts.text: I think margins can remain subdued for the company in coming quaters as well even if we assume that they are hedged completely. Margins in FoCo model are roughly 2.5%. Most new stores coming in FY27 will be FoCo only. Though it improves RoCE, margins will remain a concern and something to watch out for

posts.author: murali603
posts.text: One should be carefull with the players, who are not hedging 100% inventory, in such cases results would go either side with inventory gains/losses.

posts.author: apexcapital
posts.text: If your theory - that the company has shown inventory gains on gold as operational business profits - is correct (and I think it might be correct to some extent) then their Q1 FY27 margins won’t be good as well if gold spot price stays flat or goes further down in the month of June (gold is already trading slight negative since the start of this quarter). Whatever are the numbers going ahead, but this is dishonest behaviour by the management to give outright wrong guidance on earnings call regarding inventory hedging and allegedly using inventory profits to falsely show high operational profit…
